title: Solid-state batteries are now powering EVs in the real world
summary: Solid-state EV batteries are now being tested on the road in North America for the first time, promising longer range, faster charging, and lower costs.

title: The Chevy Equinox EV is suddenly way more expensive to lease at over $500 a month
summary: America’s most affordable EV offering over 315 miles of range, as GM calls it, just got way more expensive to lease this month.

title: Gummys go green as HARIBO turns to electric semi trucks
summary: Iconic gummy candy brand HARIBO and its transport partners at Recht Logistik are turning to electric semi trucks to deliver emissions-free shipping across several of its European operations.

title: Georgia is about to have the biggest solar cell factory in US history
summary: Solar giant Qcells has started making solar cells at its Cartersville, Georgia, factory, and it’s on track to become the largest solar cell factory in US history by Q3 2026.

title: A huge Arkansas solar + storage project locks in $3.5B in financing
summary: Cypress Creek Energy has reached a big milestone in Arkansas for one of the largest solar-plus-storage projects in the US.

title: BYD’s luxury EV roadster leaks with a tri-motor 1,500+ hp setup, and it’s headed overseas
summary: BYD is about to go head-to-head with Europe’s top luxury brands. Ahead of its official launch next month, new details reveal that the Denza Z roadster will feature a tri-motor setup, delivering a ridiculous 1,528 hp combined.

title: Bluetti exclusive Early Prime Day Elite 200 V2 power station $759, Navee G5 Max e-scooter $650 low, EGO electric mini bike, more
summary:
At the front of our Thursday Green Deals is the Early Prime Day Sale from Bluetti that benefits from two exclusive bonus savings codes for our readers, with offers like the 2,074Wh Elite 200 V2 Portable Power Station down at $759, among many others. We may not be seeing a simultaneous flash sale there like we have the last three days, but we do have Navee’s Father’s Day Sale EV lineup, like the 44-mile trekking G5 Max Electric Scooter at its $650 low for the second time ever. From there, we noticed the EGO Power+ Electric Mini Bike with 2x 7.5Ah batteries getting an extremely rare discount to $2,299, as well as a $130 discount hitting Jackery’s 512Wh Explorer 500 v2 Portable Power Station to $319, a returning low price on the Worx 20V 5-inch Pruning Saw with an extension pole bundle, and more down below. Don’t forget, we also have hangover deals collected together at the bottom of the page, like yesterday’s massive Anker SOLIX Early Prime Day power station sale lineup with exclusive bonus savings + a flash sale blitz that ends tonight, and more.

Head below for other New Green Deals we’ve found today and, of course, Electrek’s best EV buying and leasing deals. Also, check out the new Electrek Tesla Shop for the best deals on Tesla accessories.

title: Waymo launches $30/month ‘Premier’ membership with priority pickups and cashback
summary:
Waymo is launching its first-ever membership program, “Waymo Premier,” offering its most frequent riders priority pickups, 10% cashback, and early access to new cities for $29.99 per month.

The invite-only program marks a significant step in Waymo’s effort to build rider loyalty as it scales toward 1 million weekly rides and expands into 20+ cities.

title: Tesla promotes FSD misuse in its own videos — undermining its legal defense
summary:
Tesla is facing up to $14.5 billion in lawsuits related to its “Full Self-Driving” and Autopilot systems — and the company’s own marketing department keeps making the problem worse. In two official promotional videos published in the last three weeks, Tesla showed a driver making espresso while FSD drives and shared footage where FSD committed multiple traffic violations in Denmark.

The problem is obvious: Tesla’s entire legal defense in crash lawsuits rests on the argument that drivers are responsible for supervising FSD at all times. But Tesla’s own promotional content actively encourages the opposite behavior.

title: BYD’s ‘Super’ hatch goes on sale with 65 miles of EV range for under $22,000
summary: The Dolphin G DM-i is BYD’s first car specifically designed for Europe. With up to 65 miles of pure electric range and 646 miles combined, BYD said the plug-in hatch will redefine the segment as an affordable, efficient alternative. It’s now available to order, starting at under $22,000.

title: China Loves This Cheap Battery Tech. Here's Why General Motors Might Not Stick With It
summary: LFP is the industry’s go-to cheap EV battery tech, but GM says another chemistry could offer similar cost benefits with fewer drawbacks.

title: BYD's New Hybrid Hatchback Could Supercharge Its European Takeover
summary: The Dolphin G DM-i PHEV is BYD's first car designed for Europe and made in Europe.

title: BYD Says It Will Be The World's Largest Automaker In 5 Years. The Pentagon Is Nervous
summary: As BYD vows to become the largest automaker, it must also defend against allegations that it has ties to the Chinese military.

title: Mercedes’ Smaller-Battery GLC EV Doesn’t Have A Range Problem
summary: Mercedes added two lower-priced electric GLC variants, and the rear-drive version still claims up to 404 miles of WLTP range.

title: Lucid's Top Engineer Departs The EV Startup As New CEO Takes Over
summary: Emad Dlala, Lucid's senior vice president of engineering and software, has left the company, TechCrunch reports.

title: Tesla Apparently Won't Let Cybertruck Buyers Transfer FSD Without Spending Another $20k
summary: Buyers of Tesla's cheapest Cybertruck trim ordered with the intention of transferring FSD—now they can't.

title: State-Backed Mexican EV Startup Unveils $8,600 EV With 6 Seats
summary: The Olinia Uno seats six, can take a wheelchair, and is made, designed, and developed entirely in Mexico.

title: Meet The New(ish) Cheap EV Hitting The U.S. This Summer
summary: The Mitsubishi Eclipse Sportback is based on the $30,000 Nissan Leaf and hits dealerships later this year.

title: BMW’s Electric M3 Doesn’t Imitate A V8. It Does Something Cooler
summary: The goal isn’t to copy a V8, V10 or straight-six, but to give drivers the speed and acceleration cues that EVs usually lack.

title: Teslas Are More Likely To Hit 250,000 Miles Than Almost Every Car Brand
summary: Think electric cars don't last? Very few companies beat Tesla in this study of the longest lasting car brands.
